sanskrit:
अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम्।
अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय॥
transliteration: atha cittaṃ samādhātuṃ na śaknoṣi mayi sthiram | abhyāsayogena tato mām icchāptuṃ dhanañjaya ||
literal_meaning: If you are unable to steady the mind on Me, then by the yoga of repeated practice desire to reach Me, O Arjuna.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers a graded path: when effortless absorption fails, disciplined repetition becomes the bridge from scattered attention to intentional devotion.
psychological_state: Acceptance of cognitive limitation without self-condemnation; growth-mindset activation.
trigger_scenario: You sit to meditate but your mind races with tomorrow’s deadlines; instead of quitting, you commit to five minutes of breath-mantra daily, trusting the repetition itself to rewire attention.
anchor_text: Keep returning your thoughts to Me until they stay.
